title: Design System: Carnets et liste de diffusion

display.purpose: Page titles; the largest type on a screen.

body.purpose: Table cells, field values, modal copy. Cap measure at 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Field labels, badge text, column headers.

shadows.name: floating
shadows.purpose: Dropdowns, popovers, command palette. Soft ambient lift.

shadows.name: modal
shadows.purpose: Confirmation and form dialogs, including the send confirmation.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions; exponential ease-out, no bounce.

motion.name: duration-fast
motion.purpose: Color/border state changes on buttons, inputs, links.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single most important action on a screen.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: oklch(0.623 0.214 259.815); color: oklch(0.994 0.0015 264); font-family: 'Instrument Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; padding: 8px 14px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.546 0.245 262.881); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.623 0.214 259.815); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Send Button
components.description: The consequential dispatch action. Opens a confirmation stating recipient count; disabled at zero recipients.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-send"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="16" height="16" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.8" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M22 2 11 13M22 2l-7 20-4-9-9-4 20-7z"/></svg>Envoyer</button>
components.css: .ds-btn-send { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; background: oklch(0.723 0.219 149.579); color: oklch(0.994 0.0015 264); font-family: 'Instrument Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; padding: 8px 14px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-send:hover { background: oklch(0.627 0.194 149.214); } .ds-btn-send:disabled { background: oklch(0.92 0.002 286); color: oklch(0.552 0.016 285.938); cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Status Badge (Sent)
components.description: EmailStatus badge. Color always paired with a French label and icon; shown here in the 'Envoyé' (Sent) state.
components.html: Envoyé
components.css: .ds-badge-sent { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 5px; background: oklch(0.962 0.044 156.743); color: oklch(0.448 0.119 151.328); font-family: 'Instrument Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 6px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard form field. Flat surface, hairline border, blue focus ring.
components.css: .ds-input { background: oklch(0.994 0.0015 264); color: oklch(0.21 0.006 285.885); font-family: 'Instrument Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; padding: 8px 12px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.92 0.002 286); border-radius: 8px; transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: oklch(0.552 0.016 285.938); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: oklch(0.623 0.214 259.815); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.623 0.214 259.815 / 0.18); }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: Collapsible left sidebar item. Muted at rest, ink on hover, signal-blue when active.
components.html: EmailsContacts
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; padding: 8px; background: oklch(0.994 0.0015 264); border-right: 1px solid oklch(0.92 0.002 286); width: 220px; } .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 8px; font-family: 'Instrument Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.552 0.016 285.938); text-decoration: none; transition: color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-nav-item:hover { color: oklch(0.21 0.006 285.885); background: oklch(0.985 0.0015 264); } .ds-nav-item--active { color: oklch(0.546 0.245 262.881); background: oklch(0.932 0.032 255.585); }

narrative.overview: This is an instrument panel, not a marketing surface. Every screen exists so a member of a small association can maintain address books and contacts, compose a newsletter, see exactly who it will reach, and dispatch it once, with full confidence. The visual language is calm and legible: a quiet neutral field where status is always readable at a glance and color appears only where it carries meaning. Blue is the resting 'system nominal' tone; the state colors (green, amber, red) light up only to report what is happening to a campaign. The system is built on Filament v5 and embraces its restrained component vocabulary: flat surfaces layered tonally, a single humanist sans, generous spacing. The one dramatic moment, the irreversible send, is gated behind a confirmation that states the recipient count before it commits.
keyCharacteristics: Calm neutral baseline; color reserved for state and the primary action.
Flat surfaces, depth from tonal layering and hairline borders, not shadows.
A single humanist sans (Instrument Sans) across the whole hierarchy.
State is never color-only: badge color is always paired with a French label.
The send path shows its consequence (recipient count) before it commits.

rules.name: The Meaning-Only Color Rule
rules.body: Color is forbidden as decoration. Every saturated color on screen must report a state, mark the one primary action, or flag a destructive action. If a color is not carrying meaning, it must be neutral.

rules.name: The Signal-Blue-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: Blue is the calm baseline, not an alarm. Urgency is expressed by amber and red on state, never by flooding the screen with primary.

rules.name: The One-Voice Type Rule
rules.body: One family does all the work. Never introduce a second display or serif face to create emphasis; raise the weight or the size instead.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. A shadow is a response to a floating state (open menu, open modal), never a default decoration on a card.

dos: Do keep color meaningful: reserve saturated color for status badges, the one primary action, and destructive actions.
Do pair every status color with its French label and icon; never communicate state by color alone.
Do gate every irreversible action behind a confirmation that states its consequence before it commits.
Do keep surfaces flat at rest; reach for shadow only on floating menus and modals.
Do tint neutrals faintly toward the blue hue and use Ink/Surface instead of pure black or pure white.
Do maintain a visible 2px Signal Blue focus ring on all interactive elements and respect prefers-reduced-motion.
donts: Don't build a generic SaaS dashboard: no hero-metric template, no identical icon-heading-text card grids, no gradients standing in for hierarchy.
Don't drift toward a loud consumer marketing email tool: no busy multi-color screens, mascots, or personality that competes with clarity.
Don't become a cold enterprise back-office: no sterile all-gray density that intimidates non-technical users.
Don't use border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't use gradient text or glassmorphism anywhere.
Don't use pure #000 or #fff; use Ink and Surface.
Don't flood a screen with primary blue to signal urgency; that is amber's and red's job, on state only.
